perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement with respect to the Confidential Information disclosed herein and super sedes all prior or contemporaneous oral or written agreements concerning such Confidential Information . This Agreement may only be changed by mutual agreement between the parties in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes an initial understanding with respect to the Confidential Information disclosed herein and is subject to revisions based on the Society's discretion. Changes to this Agreement do not require written consent and can be communicated verbally.
perturbation.explanation: This change weakens the 'Entire Agreement' clause. The original text establishes that the contract represents the complete understanding, while the modified version suggests that it's only an initial understanding and that the Society can make changes without written consent. This creates uncertainty about the contract's binding nature.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes an initial understanding with respect to the Confidential Information disclosed herein and is subject to revisions based on the Society's discretion. Changes to this Agreement do not require written consent and can be communicated verbally.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the agreement is only an initial understanding subject to revision, and changes do not require written consent and can be communicated verbally. This directly contradicts the concept of the original 'Entire Agreement' clause which intended that the agreement represents the complete and final understanding between parties and could only be changed with mutual written agreement. Because the contradicted text is the same as the changed text, the contradiction is clear.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient will promptly deliver to the Soc iety, without retaining any copies, all documents a nd other materials furnished to Recipient by the Society.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient will endeavor to promptly deliver to the Society all documents and other materials furnished to Recipient by the Society.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the word 'will' to 'will endeavor,' the obligation to return documents is weakened. This introduces uncertainty because 'endeavor' implies an attempt, but not a guarantee. This contradicts the spirit of protecting confidential information because the recipient is no longer absolutely obligated to return materials.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient will maintain in confidence and will no t disclose, disseminate or use any Confidential Infor mation belonging to and/or related to Society, whet her or not in written form. Recipient agrees that Recipient s hall treat all Confidential Information of the Soci ety with at least the same degree of care as Recipient accords its own confidential information. Recipient furthe r represents that Recipient exercises at least reason able care to protect its own confidential informati on.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the recipient 'will' promptly deliver documents, creating a firm obligation. The changed text weakens this obligation to 'will endeavor,' suggesting an attempt but not a guarantee. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which requires the recipient to 'maintain in confidence and will not disclose' confidential information and treat it with the same degree of care as their own. The weaker obligation to return documents undermines the absolute confidentiality requirement, because the recipient may not return all materials, potentially compromising confidential information. The change introduces uncertainty and potential non-compliance, directly conflicting with the firm confidentiality obligations, thus it is a Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: A breach of any of the promises or agreements co ntained herein will result in irreparable and continuing damage to the Society fo r which there will be no adequate remedy at law, an d the Society shall be entitled to injunctive relief and/ or a decree for specific performance, and such othe r relief as may be proper (including monetary damages if approp riate).
perturbation.changed_text: A breach of any of the promises or agreements contained herein may, at the sole discretion of the Society, result in damage. The Society may consider injunctive relief, but there is no guarantee of such remedy, and monetary damages are explicitly excluded.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause grants the Society a strong legal recourse (injunctive relief and/or damages) for breaches. The revised clause weakens this by making remedies discretionary and excluding monetary damages. The term 'may' instead of 'will' creates uncertainty about the consequences of a breach and its impact on the agreement's enforceability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient will maintain in confidence and will no t disclose, disseminate or use any Confidential Infor mation belonging to and/or related to Society, whet her or not in written form. Recipient agrees that Recipient s hall treat all Confidential Information of the Soci ety with at least the same degree of care as Recipient accords its own confidential information. Recipient furthe r represents that Recipient exercises at least reason able care to protect its own confidential informati on.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated that breaches would result in specific legal remedies (injunctive relief and/or monetary damages). The changed text makes these remedies discretionary and explicitly excludes monetary damages. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which implicitly relies on the availability of remedies to protect confidential information belonging to the Society. The changed text weakens the Society's ability to enforce confidentiality, thus creating a significant in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed in all respects by the laws of the Commonwealth of Australia and by the laws of the St ate of Victoria; as such laws are applied to agreem ents entered into and to be performed entirely within Au stralia.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be interpreted according to principles of fairness and good faith, rather than strictly adhering to the laws of the Commonwealth of Australia and the State of Victoria.
perturbation.explanation: The change replaces the governing law with principles of fairness and good faith. This could create confusion because 'fairness and good faith' are subjective standards compared to established legal frameworks. It is unclear which legal principles will be used if a dispute arises, contradicting the purpose of having a governing law clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A breach of any of the promises or agreements co ntained herein will result in irreparable and continuing damage to the Society fo r which there will be no adequate remedy at law, an d the Society shall be entitled to injunctive relief and/ or a decree for specific performance, and such othe r relief as may be proper (including monetary damages if approp riate).
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the agreement is governed by the laws of the Commonwealth of Australia and the State of Victoria. The changed text replaces this with 'principles of fairness and good faith,' which are subjective standards. The contradicted text states that breaches will result in irreparable damage to the Society for which there will be no adequate remedy at law, entitling the Society to injunctive relief, specific performance, and other relief. By removing the governing law and replacing it with subjective principles, it becomes unclear which legal framework to use when determining if a breach occurred or what remedies are available, creating a direct conflict and contradiction with the contradicted section. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition, where conflicting implications arise from the changed clause.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees that Recipient s hall treat all Confidential Information of the Soci ety with at least the same degree of care as Recipient accords its own confidential information. Recipient furthe r represents that Recipient exercises at least reason able care to protect its own confidential informati on.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient acknowledges that Society's Confidential Information may be accessed by Recipient, but it is up to the discretion of the recipient if they want to protect it or not.
perturbation.explanation: The new definition is a direct opposite of the original text. The first definition establishes Recipient is going to maintain confidence as they do with their own information. The modified version allows the recipient to do whatever they want with the confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient acknowledges that Society's Confidential Information may be accessed by Recipient, but it is up to the discretion of the recipient if they want to protect it or not.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Recipient must treat the Society's confidential information with the same degree of care as their own, implying a duty of protection. The changed text, however, explicitly allows the Recipient to choose whether or not to protect the Society's confidential information. This creates a direct contradiction. The contradicted text section is identical to the changed text, reinforcing the contradiction. The explanation also clearly states that the new definition is a direct opposite of the original text, making it a strong contradiction.
